description: A half-length portrait of Saint Euphemia depicted as a young woman with a serene, downward-cast gaze. She has a thin golden halo around her head. She wears a deep red bodice beneath a voluminous blue-grey mantle draped over her shoulders and arms. Her right hand rests gently on her chest in a gesture of devotion, while her left hand holds a tall dark staff or palm branch -- the attribute of martyrdom. The background is a dark, neutral tone that focuses attention on the saint's calm, dignified figure.
theological_description: Saint Euphemia of Chalcedon (c. 289-303 AD) was an early Christian martyr venerated for her steadfast faith during the Diocletian persecutions. The palm branch she holds is the traditional symbol of martyrdom, echoing the vision in Revelation of the great multitude who came through tribulation standing before the throne with palm branches. Her gesture of hand upon heart signifies interior faith and devotion. Though a post-biblical figure, her witness embodies the call to faithfulness unto death promised a crown of life, and the apostolic exhortation to endure suffering for the sake of Christ.
